qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: IP whitelist bypass via X-Forwarded-For header spoofing in whitelist middleware
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an IP whitelist bypass in the whitelist middleware (whitelist/ip.go) via X-Forwarded-For header iteration. The advisory describes a StripPrefixRegex middleware Path/RawPath byte-length desync (pkg/middlewares/stripprefixregex/strip_prefix_regex.go:62). These are different middlewares, different root causes, and different vulnerability classes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: StripPrefixRegex middleware authorization bypass via Path/RawPath desync in pkg/middlewares/stripprefixregex/strip_prefix_regex.go:62. The ip_whitelist_xff_bypass finding describes a different middleware (whitelist/ip.go) with a different root cause (X-Forwarded-For iteration logic).
evidence: Advisory root cause: StripPrefixRegex middleware uses decoded Path match byte length to slice encoded RawPath (pkg/middlewares/stripprefixregex/strip_prefix_regex.go:62), causing dot-segment confusion that bypasses ForwardAuth/BasicAuth/DigestAuth.
ip_whitelist_xff_bypass: Different middleware (whitelist/ip.go), different root cause (iterates all X-Forwarded-For entries instead of rightmost only), different vulnerable component.
